theme: journeymen matched to masters with side payments
title: Journeymen, Masters, and Sweeteners
seed_problem: bipartite stable matching (Gale-Shapley) on cardinal utilities
mutation: composite: replace the decision goal (find A stable matching) with a graded objective (maximize welfare minus a priced penalty per blocking pair), and add a bounded per-pair side-payment channel that can locally re-price which pairs actually block -- so a matching's stability becomes a spendable, capped resource instead of a hard constraint.
strategies: trivial: greedy-by-input-order matching, zero payments (reproduces the checker baseline)
greedy: sort edges by combined utility a+b descending and augment a matching greedily; never pays -- chases raw welfare and drowns in blocking penalties on contested-star tests

strong: build the same weight-sorted matching, then compute the zero-payment envy boundary (which matched pairs are actually threatened) and spend each pair's sweetener cap only there, favoring whichever side clears more blocking edges per unit of cap, with a second pass for knock-on exposure -- payments a pure transfer, welfare unchanged, blocking count driven down surgically
invalid: claims more matched pairs than min(n,m) allows -- infeasible on every test
